policy.amendmentRule: A migration may be corrected in place only when the repository has no successful same-commit runtime evidence for it, clean installation or invariant verification proves the file is unsafe, and a governed forward-recovery migration exists for any environment that may have recorded the previous checksum.

amendments.reason: The original table-level UNIQUE(client_id, create_idempotency_key) constraint covered soft-deleted rows and contradicted the address lifecycle invariant that a deleted operation releases its idempotency key while active rows remain conflict-safe.
amendments.requiredCorrection: Replace full-history uniqueness with uq_dsh_client_addresses_active_idempotency WHERE deleted_at IS NULL and retain dsh-900_client_address_active_idempotency_recovery.sql as the explicit forward recovery for any environment that applied the original constraint.

amendments.reason: The migration declared TEXT foreign keys against the UUID zone identity created by dsh-013 and introduced a second capacity table instead of upgrading the legacy capacity source.
amendments.requiredCorrection: Preserve UUID zone identity, migrate legacy capacity rows into the governed capacity-config table, and remove the duplicate legacy table only after successful copy.

amendments.reason: The migration declared settlement_id UUID while the sovereign wlt_settlements primary key has always been TEXT with a wset_ prefix.
amendments.requiredCorrection: Use the exact sovereign settlement identifier type and retain the existing restrictive foreign key.
